name: Amhari- Mesir Wat (Ethiopian Lentil Bowl)
recipeInstructions:
This recipe is common to the Ethiopian Jews (Falashas). My understanding is that these are NOT unique to them.
1. Sort the lentils and soak in tap water for 30 minutes. Rinse in running water and drain.
2. Peel and finely chop the onions. Peel and mince the garlic.
3. Heat the oil in a large pan and sauté the onion until golden brown.
4. Add tomato paste, paprika, minced garlic, ground ginger, black pepper, and salt to the pan. Mix well.
5. Add half the water and stir.
6. Gradually add the remaining water, stirring again after each addition.
7. Cover and bring the mixture to a boil over medium heat.
8. Reduce the flame to low once boiling and add the lentils.
9. Cook for 20-30 minutes or until the lentils are tender. Serve hot.
